results.title: Mixed drum audio to MIDI
results.content:
##### Gastronomer

   Sep 1, 2025

   
   #6

I just bought DrumsSSX since it's on sale for $15 at PB. It's a very cool plugin, and the manual actually explains how the algorithm works, which is quite interesting to see. 

 The downside: it's quite tricky to use as a MIDI trigger because it doesn't show up as an instrument, so you need to manually bounce the channels out, and then convert them each to MIDI with something like Melodyne. Plus, the separation isn't very clean, so you might need to gate the results. 

 But one thing which I didn't notice until trying it is that it doesn't seem to support hi-hats. It can do kicks+snares+toms+cymbals, but without hi-hats it's not quite as helpful as I had envisioned.

   Image 17: Like

Reactions:oepion and Gaffable

S

> Click to expand... [...] Thanks for bringing up Sonnox's Drum Gate, I forgot about that one and it would be awesome if they bring the hihat detection feature to a future version as that would be significantly cheaper than buying SD3.

 I've tried to avoid stem separation because while it would allow easier detection of hits from the other plugins, it adds an additional step (and potential cost if the stem separation tool isn't free). So it's a little bit more cumbersome, though definitely a valid method and probably more efficient than manual adjustments on Melodyne.

results.title: Drum2Notes - Turn Drums Music into Notes
results.content:
## Drums are not your Thing? No Problem!

### Check out our other transcription apps:

### Piano2Notes

Turn Pianos into Notes.

### Guitar2Tabs

Turn Acoustic, Electirc and Bass Guitars into Notes.

### Sing2Notes

Turn Singing into Notes.

### Melody Scanner

Transform any Song into Piano Arrangements.

## Known from



How does Drum2Notes work?

Drum2Notes can turn the drums of any song into notes, within seconds! Simply upload an audio file, paste in a YouTube link or record your instrument directly. Our AI then listens to the drums and transcribes them automatically into notes, which are displayed as sheet music. You can then edit your transcription and download it in different formats.

What kind of music can Drum2Notes transcribe? [...] Find out more in this article.

Is Drum2Notes for free?

You can do as many Demo Transcriptions with Drum2Notes as you like, which are completely for free! This allows you to test the quality before upgrading to full transcriptions, which also unlock many more features!

Find out more in this article.

How precise does Drum2Notes transcribe music?

The better the quality of the music, the better the transcription – it’s as simple as that! In other words, if a professional transcriber will have a hard time to transcribe a certain part, then so will Drum2Notes. But even if there are some mistakes in your transcribed notes, you’ve saved tons of time with Drum2Notes and the Edit Mode lets you fix these mistakes with a couple clicks.

Find out more in this article.

## Get in touch with us! [...] Skip to content

results.title: ReStem + Logic Pro: Convert Any Drum Loop to MIDI with AI
results.content: highhat hits in the audio file. If not, we can adjust the threshold slider, but looks like we're good here. We can then change the trigger note to match the closed high hat sound in drum machine designer. That's F# one. Hit okay. And then we can load a drum machine designer patch or use any drum software instrument we like. Do the same to the kick and snare. And boom, loop is converted to MIDI. So cool. [Music] Now we're free to edit this to our heart's content to make a new pattern that's completely unique for our song. [Music] Or we could take this whole thing to the next level by chopping out a sample of the audio, kick, snare, and high hat stems and then loading them into drum machine designer individually.
